title: National Coach
description:
Job Description & Requirements

Singapore Silat Federation is seeking a suitable candidate to fill the position of National Coach, Match and Artistic. If you have the competencies and passion to lead elite Silat athletes to compete at World, Asia and South-East Asia level, we want you.

  • You will work together with a team of coaches to plan and implement the training and competition plans for the athletes. You will conduct regular discussions with the athletes on their progress and goals and evaluate the plans for improvement.
  • You will be expected to provide technical expertise and mentorship, to guide and develop the team of coaches under you, for their growth and development, and the achievement of the Federation's goals. You will work with the Technical Team and coaches to identify and recruit suitable athletes, and develop them towards eventual national team representation.
  • You will work with Sport Singapore (SportSG)/Singapore Sport Institute (SSI), National Youth Sports Institute (NYSI), to plan for the relevant development and performance support services required for the different levels of athletes.
  • Where needed, you will also work with other coaches and clubs in the Silat community to ensure the achievement of the Federation's goals.

If you are a highly motivated, engaging and committed team leader with a good understanding of the needs of elite athletes, and if you value growth and excellence in a challenging environment, we invite you to join us.


Requirements


• At least 5 years of competitive coaching experience with elite and developmental youth level, and possesses strong working knowledge in all categories

• Preferably possesses an internationally recognized high level coaching qualification

• Preferably possesses at least SG-Coach Level 1 (Full) certification or equivalent, and is registered with the National Registry of Coaches (NROC). • Experience in leading and managing a team of coaches

• Displays good understanding of sports science on long term athlete development principles

• Competent in values inculcation and character development for athletes

• Good planning, administrative and organizational skills

• Able to communicate well in English with athletes and stakeholders

postedCompany.description:
The Federation

Singapore Silat Federations formed in 1976 and is registered with the Registry

of Societies since 25 June.


Our Mission

To be the beacon for Pencak Silat in Singapore, promoting it as a wholesome

sport for ALL and nurturing those with the attributes for “competitive Silat” to

realize their maximum potential.


Our Vision

Pencak Silat, a mainstream and widely participated sport in multi-racial

Singapore, and an important contributor of regional and international awards

and accolades to Singapore sports.
postedCompany.name: Singapore Silat Federation

districts.location: D16 Upper East Coast, Bedok, Eastwood, Kew Drive
